title: First Flight camera, movement, and morph behavior contract candidate
scope: A public-safe acceptance shape for the smallest retail observations that can improve First Flight movement without treating static identity or reference source as runtime behavior proof.

evidenceAuthority.sourceHypothesis: Stuart source may supply architecture, naming, and expected control-flow hypotheses only.
evidenceAuthority.steamStaticCorroboration: Reviewed Steam-static evidence may establish bounded released-code identity, ownership, and topology only.
evidenceAuthority.copiedRuntimeMeasurement: Controlled observations of a verified copied executable are required for player-visible behavior, values, direction, timing, and causality.
evidenceAuthority.rebuild: The rebuild consumes an accepted contract and cannot corroborate retail behavior by agreeing with itself.

provenance.copiedRuntimeEvidence: none accepted for these observation rows

sourceHypothesis.summary: Movement intent may be transformed through the active view or actor frame before reaching BattleEngine movement.

steamStaticCorroboration.summary: Camera access and mouse-look routines are identified, while the reviewed movement cluster establishes a separate BattleEngine movement owner; their player-visible directional relationship is not statically proven.

requiredObservations: Hold the same single directional input from two clearly separated camera headings in otherwise matched fresh runs.
Record camera forward/right axes and actor displacement direction at input start and after a short stable window.
Retain only normalized vectors, event order, and identity/integrity results in public evidence.

tolerances.acceptanceRule: Set angular and displacement tolerances only from repeat copied-runtime observations; do not infer them from rebuild output or source constants.

rebuildRequirement.requirement: Use the accepted retail reference frame for directional intent while keeping camera presentation outside deterministic simulation truth.

nonclaims: No camera-relative movement behavior is proven yet.
No FOV, sensitivity, smoothing, inversion, or camera timing value is claimed.

sourceHypothesis.summary: Walker movement should respond directionally to transformed intent and update walk-cycle state from local velocity.

steamStaticCorroboration.summary: BattleEngine dispatches the walker path, and the accepted walker helper projects velocity into local space for walk-cycle advancement.

requiredObservations: From a stable walker state, apply one forward or lateral input without morphing.
Record normalized input, actor basis, displacement direction, speed samples, grounded state, and event timestamps.
Repeat enough matched runs to distinguish response direction from incidental terrain drift.

tolerances.acceptanceRule: Derive directional, latency, and speed envelopes from accepted repeats on bounded terrain; no single run freezes a constant.

rebuildRequirement.requirement: Match the accepted walker response direction and bounded response envelope without importing presentation animation into Core.

nonclaims: No walker acceleration, top speed, turn rate, animation phase, or terrain-friction value is established.
Static walk-cycle ownership is not runtime handling proof.

sourceHypothesis.summary: Jet movement should combine transformed intent with flight, near-ground, and friction behavior owned by the JetPart path.

steamStaticCorroboration.summary: The accepted JetPart movement owner reaches ground-effect and friction helpers, but static topology does not establish player-visible response values.

requiredObservations: Begin from a stable verified jet state and apply one directional input over bounded terrain.
Record normalized input, camera and actor bases, displacement and velocity directions, altitude band, grounded state, and event timestamps.
Use separate matched observations for ordinary flight and near-ground behavior before attributing a ground-effect difference.

tolerances.acceptanceRule: Derive direction, latency, speed, and altitude-band envelopes from accepted repeats; keep ordinary-flight and near-ground tolerances separate.

rebuildRequirement.requirement: Match only accepted jet directional-response envelopes; treat ground effect and friction as separate requirements when evidence supports them.

nonclaims: No jet acceleration, top speed, energy cost, stall, friction, or ground-effect constant is established.
Accepted helper identities do not establish runtime magnitude or causality.

sourceHypothesis.summary: A transform action requests a BattleEngine morph whose success or rejection changes the movement mode reached by later updates.

steamStaticCorroboration.summary: The player action receiver, BattleEngine morph owner, and corrected movement owners form a candidate request-to-update path; no accepted runtime correlation exists.

requiredObservations: After simpler camera and single-mode movement observations, correlate one exact transform request with observable mode before and after the request.
Record success or rejection, transition start/end if observable, energy, shield, velocity, grounded and stall state, and event timestamps.
Use a new bounded observation design; the prior three-role Retry 13 is not authorized.

tolerances.acceptanceRule: Establish categorical success/rejection and timing/state tolerances only after an accepted, repeatable copied-runtime observation.

rebuildRequirement.requirement: Replace synthetic instant switching only when an accepted contract defines request outcome, observable transition, state effects, and tolerances.

nonclaims: No transform success condition, duration, cost, cooldown, state enum, or rejection cause is established.
No animation, audio, effect, weapon eligibility, or mission interaction is claimed.

globalNonclaims: This candidate is not an accepted retail behavior contract and authorizes no rebuild change.
The 6,411-function metadata audit and reviewed correction wave are not parity proof.
No whole-game, handling, camera, controls, weapon, mission, animation, audio, or presentation parity is claimed.
